title: Implement lenient Excalidraw file and element data model
description:
Implement excalidraw-core serde models for ExcalidrawFile, AppState, FileData, BaseElement, concrete element structs, unsupported elements, and the Element enum. The parser must be lenient with optional/version-dependent fields, preserve unknown top-level and element fields, and retain unknown element raw JSON so future Excalidraw versions remain debuggable. Supported known types are rectangle, ellipse, diamond, arrow, line, freedraw, text, image, frame, magicframe, embeddable, and iframe.

## Acceptance Criteria

Realistic .excalidraw JSON deserializes without unnecessary failure; missing optional fields get documented defaults; unknown fields are preserved; unknown element types become Element::Unknown with raw JSON; embeddable/iframe remain unsupported placeholder-capable data, not fatal errors; unit tests cover missing fields and unknown fields.
acceptance_criteria: Realistic .excalidraw JSON deserializes without unnecessary failure; missing optional fields get documented defaults; unknown fields are preserved; unknown element types become Element::Unknown with raw JSON; embeddable/iframe remain unsupported placeholder-capable data, not fatal errors; unit tests cover missing fields and unknown fields.
close_reason: Implemented lenient Excalidraw serde data model with top-level/app-state/file/base/concrete/unsupported/unknown element types, raw unknown-field preservation, and focused model tests.

title: Implement precise bounds for shapes, text, arrows, images, frames, and unsupported placeholders
description:
Implement bounds calculations for element dimensions, linear points, rotation around element center, stroke width expansion, roughness safety margin, arrowhead extents, measured text, frame labels, image crop visibility, and unsupported placeholders. Produce both content_bounds and export_bounds, where export bounds include render padding.

## Acceptance Criteria

Bounds tests cover rotation, strokes, roughness margin, arrows and arrowheads, text, cropped/flipped images, frame labels, and unsupported placeholders; content_bounds and export_bounds are distinct and documented; renderer can rely on bounds without recomputing scene structure.
acceptance_criteria: Bounds tests cover rotation, strokes, roughness margin, arrows and arrowheads, text, cropped/flipped images, frame labels, and unsupported placeholders; content_bounds and export_bounds are distinct and documented; renderer can rely on bounds without recomputing scene structure.
close_reason: Implemented normalized visual bounds for shapes, linear arrows, text, images, frames, freedraw, unsupported placeholders, rotation, content bounds, export padding, and focused bounds tests.

title: Implement colors, enums, defaults, and compatibility helpers
description:
Add default functions and compatibility helpers for stroke/background colors, fill style, stroke style, roughness, opacity, font size, font family, text alignment, vertical alignment, line height, roundness, arrowheads, and bindings. Color parsing should support Excalidraw-style colors and report invalid values as warnings instead of causing render panics where possible.

## Acceptance Criteria

All planned defaults are centralized and tested; enum unknown variants deserialize safely; invalid colors can be surfaced as validation/render warnings; font family CSS mapping is versioned and matches the plan; no consumer crate reimplements these helpers.
acceptance_criteria: All planned defaults are centralized and tested; enum unknown variants deserialize safely; invalid colors can be surfaced as validation/render warnings; font family CSS mapping is versioned and matches the plan; no consumer crate reimplements these helpers.
close_reason: Centralized Excalidraw defaults, exposed versioned font-family mapping, added structured color parser/errors, and covered defaults/enums/color behavior with tests.

title: Add CLI integration and end-to-end render tests
description:
Add integration tests for the excd binary using fixture files. Tests should validate command exit codes, stdout/file output behavior, warning modes, malformed input handling, quality/background options, and convert extension inference. Keep logs detailed enough for future agent debugging.

## Acceptance Criteria

CLI tests run with cargo test; to-svg stdout and file output are covered; to-png default output behavior is covered in a temp dir; validate/info/describe return stable machine-readable data; malformed input produces useful errors; test logs include command, fixture, and output path.
acceptance_criteria: CLI tests run with cargo test; to-svg stdout and file output are covered; to-png default output behavior is covered in a temp dir; validate/info/describe return stable machine-readable data; malformed input produces useful errors; test logs include command, fixture, and output path.

title: Implement Excalidraw-compatible z-order and fractional index fallback
description:
Implement z-order sorting that uses valid Excalidraw fractional indexes when available and falls back to stable original array order when indexes are missing or invalid. Do not use naive string sorting unless it has been verified against Excalidraw fractional index semantics. Invalid indexes should produce warnings and deterministic fallback behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Fixtures with valid fractional indexes render in expected order; missing/invalid indexes use stable original order; mixed validity produces documented warnings; deterministic tests prevent ordering drift; behavior is isolated in core, not renderer consumers.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures with valid fractional indexes render in expected order; missing/invalid indexes use stable original order; mixed validity produces documented warnings; deterministic tests prevent ordering drift; behavior is isolated in core, not renderer consumers.
close_reason: Implemented Excalidraw fractional-index z-order normalization with validated order keys, stable fallback warnings, duplicate detection, id-map rebuild after render sorting, and deterministic tests.

title: Add normalization snapshot tests for relationships and bounds
description:
Add structural snapshot tests for normalized z-order, id maps, frame child mappings, bound text mappings, arrow binding mappings, absolute points, bounds, scene background, and warnings. These tests should make renderer regressions easier to isolate from parser/normalizer regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria

Snapshots/assertions cover simple, complex, frame, bound text, bound arrow, image, and large fixtures; failed tests identify the normalization artifact that changed; no renderer code is needed for these tests.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshots/assertions cover simple, complex, frame, bound text, bound arrow, image, and large fixtures; failed tests identify the normalization artifact that changed; no renderer code is needed for these tests.

title: Add Mermaid CLI and MCP tools for v0.2
description:
Add excd mermaid-to-excalidraw and excd mermaid commands plus the MCP tool mermaid_to_excalidraw. Commands should accept files or stdin, output .excalidraw/SVG/PNG based on format, optionally render, and return warnings, element counts, diagram type, and optional png_base64 in MCP responses.

## Acceptance Criteria

CLI supports excd mermaid-to-excalidraw input.mmd output.excalidraw, excd mermaid input.mmd output.svg/png/excalidraw, and stdin; MCP mermaid_to_excalidraw response fields match the plan; tests cover parse, conversion snapshot, render SVG validity, visual regression after render, and unsupported fallback.
acceptance_criteria: CLI supports excd mermaid-to-excalidraw input.mmd output.excalidraw, excd mermaid input.mmd output.svg/png/excalidraw, and stdin; MCP mermaid_to_excalidraw response fields match the plan; tests cover parse, conversion snapshot, render SVG validity, visual regression after render, and unsupported fallback.

title: Implement terminal viewer using shared PNG rendering
description:
Implement excalidraw-tui as a consumer of core/render. It should render PNG through the shared pipeline, detect terminal image protocol in priority order Kitty, Sixel, iTerm2, then halfblock fallback, and support pan/zoom/save interactions. Dark-mode or terminal theme display choices must not mutate source scene colors unless explicitly requested.

## Acceptance Criteria

excd view opens the TUI; protocol detection is isolated and tested where possible; q/Esc quit, +/- zoom, hjkl/arrows pan, r resets, s saves PNG, e saves SVG; tmux/WSL2 notes are documented; no renderer logic is duplicated in TUI.
acceptance_criteria: excd view opens the TUI; protocol detection is isolated and tested where possible; q/Esc quit, +/- zoom, hjkl/arrows pan, r resets, s saves PNG, e saves SVG; tmux/WSL2 notes are documented; no renderer logic is duplicated in TUI.

title: Build parse compatibility and structural snapshot tests
description:
Create focused tests that prove parse compatibility across official exports, generated files, old/missing-field files, images, frames, bound text, arrows, unsupported elements, and unknown fields. Add structural snapshots for parsed element counts, type lists, warnings, and preserved raw fields so regressions are obvious.

## Acceptance Criteria

cargo test exercises all fixture categories; snapshots or equivalent assertions capture element counts/types/warnings; parser tests require no renderer; tests prove unknown fields survive parse; failure messages identify the fixture and expected compatibility behavior.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test exercises all fixture categories; snapshots or equivalent assertions capture element counts/types/warnings; parser tests require no renderer; tests prove unknown fields survive parse; failure messages identify the fixture and expected compatibility behavior.

title: Implement excalidraw-mermaid crate and public conversion API
description:
Create the v0.2 excalidraw-mermaid crate with engine, error, style, fallback, and convert modules. Expose MermaidConvertOptions, FlowchartCurve, OnUnsupported, parse_to_excalidraw, and parse_to_excalidraw_file. It should consume merman layout output and produce Excalidraw elements that are rendered by the existing v0.1 renderer, without disturbing core rendering semantics.

## Success Criteria

Crate compiles as a workspace member; public API matches the plan; dependency versions are exactly pinned; conversion returns Excalidraw elements/files; unsupported diagrams can error or produce placeholders according to options; v0.1 renderer APIs remain stable.
acceptance_criteria: Crate compiles as a workspace member; public API matches the plan; dependency versions are exactly pinned; conversion returns Excalidraw elements/files; unsupported diagrams can error or produce placeholders according to options; v0.1 renderer APIs remain stable.

title: Implement text measurement, font registration, SVG text, and bound text
description:
Implement the text subsystem: font family mapping, bundled/shared font registration for SVG and PNG paths, measured bounds, multi-line layout, fontSize/fontFamily/lineHeight, horizontal and vertical alignment, standalone text, container-bound text, and arrow label text. Text is a high-fidelity risk and should remain SVG <text> in v0.1 while keeping a future path conversion policy possible.

## Acceptance Criteria

Standalone and bound text render with correct alignment; multiline line-height is honored; bounds are measured rather than guessed; PNG and SVG use the same font registration source; arrow labels render as bound text; tests cover font families, vertical/horizontal alignment, multiline text, container text, and missing container fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Standalone and bound text render with correct alignment; multiline line-height is honored; bounds are measured rather than guessed; PNG and SVG use the same font registration source; arrow labels render as bound text; tests cover font families, vertical/horizontal alignment, multiline text, container text, and missing container fallback.

title: Implement line and arrow rendering with stored points and explicit arrowheads
description:
Render line and arrow elements from stored point arrays without rerouting, including multi-point paths, dashed/dotted styles, elbow arrows using stored points, and binding metadata for descriptions. Implement explicit SVG arrowhead geometry for arrow, triangle, triangle_outline, bar, dot, circle, diamond, and crowfoot, following endpoint tangent direction, stroke color, opacity, stroke width, and scale.

## Acceptance Criteria

Lines/arrows render from abs_points; no editor-like rerouting occurs; all planned arrowhead types have tests; arrowheads are included in bounds; dash/dot style works; binding metadata is preserved for describe/MCP but does not override stored geometry.
acceptance_criteria: Lines/arrows render from abs_points; no editor-like rerouting occurs; all planned arrowhead types have tests; arrowheads are included in bounds; dash/dot style works; binding metadata is preserved for describe/MCP but does not override stored geometry.

title: Implement images, frames, freedraw, and unsupported placeholders
description:
Complete the remaining v0.1 element renderers. Images pass data URLs through for SVG, warn and render placeholders on missing data, support crop via clipPath and transform, and support scale flips. Frames render border/name labels, optionally clip children, and handle collapsed/nested frames without recursion loops. Freedraw uses a perfect-freehand-compatible stroke outline with pressure and simulatePressure support. Unsupported/unknown elements render placeholders by default and preserve warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria

Image dataURL, missing image, crop, and flip fixtures render; frames clip children only when clip is true; collapsed/nested frames do not panic; freedraw pressure and simulatePressure have tests; unsupported/unknown elements produce visible placeholders and warnings unless policy says skip/error.
acceptance_criteria: Image dataURL, missing image, crop, and flip fixtures render; frames clip children only when clip is true; collapsed/nested frames do not panic; freedraw pressure and simulatePressure have tests; unsupported/unknown elements produce visible placeholders and warnings unless policy says skip/error.

title: Spike Mermaid parser/layout integration before v0.2 conversion
description:
Before implementing Mermaid conversion, prove the v0.2 gate: merman-core parses target fixtures, merman-render layout works, text measurer compiles, at least one flowchart converts into valid Excalidraw elements, generated .excalidraw renders through the v0.1 renderer, and the v0.1 renderer API does not need breaking changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

Spike pins merman-core and merman-render exact versions; flowchart_simple.mmd converts to a valid .excalidraw file; generated file renders through render_svg/render_png; findings document API risks and whether v0.1 changes are needed; unsupported diagram behavior is understood.
acceptance_criteria: Spike pins merman-core and merman-render exact versions; flowchart_simple.mmd converts to a valid .excalidraw file; generated file renders through render_svg/render_png; findings document API risks and whether v0.1 changes are needed; unsupported diagram behavior is understood.

title: Prepare v0.1 crates.io and GitHub release
description:
Prepare the first release once core, renderer, CLI, TUI, MCP, tests, and fidelity pass are stable. Publish order is excalidraw-core, excalidraw-render, excalidraw-tui, excalidraw-mcp, then excalidraw-cli. Switch rough-rs from local path dependency to the published version for release. Update README, API docs, CHANGELOG, and release binaries.

## Success Criteria

README usage is accurate; public API docs build; CHANGELOG captures v0.1 scope and known deviations; crates publish in dependency order; rough-rs dependency is release-ready; GitHub release contains excd binaries and verification notes.
acceptance_criteria: README usage is accurate; public API docs build; CHANGELOG captures v0.1 scope and known deviations; crates publish in dependency order; rough-rs dependency is release-ready; GitHub release contains excd binaries and verification notes.

title: Implement PNG rendering through resvg/usvg/tiny-skia
description:
Implement render_png by passing the generated SVG through usvg/resvg/tiny-skia with shared fontdb setup. PNG rendering must honor scale, padding, background modes, transparent background, embedded images, and text fonts. SVG remains the source of truth so PNG behavior does not fork rendering semantics.

## Acceptance Criteria

render_png returns PNG bytes plus warnings; scale changes output dimensions predictably; transparent and from-file backgrounds work; same font database is used for SVG/PNG text; PNG tests rasterize simple, text, image, and complex fixtures; no browser or Node dependency is introduced.
acceptance_criteria: render_png returns PNG bytes plus warnings; scale changes output dimensions predictably; transparent and from-file backgrounds work; same font database is used for SVG/PNG text; PNG tests rasterize simple, text, image, and complex fixtures; no browser or Node dependency is introduced.

title: Implement Scene normalization pipeline
description:
Implement normalize() from parsed ExcalidrawFile into Scene and NormalizedElement. The pipeline filters deleted elements, preserves original order, computes render order, builds id_map, creates container text and bound element indexes, groups frame children, converts linear relative points to absolute points, computes element bounds, and records scene-level warnings. This is the central contract consumed by render, CLI, TUI, and MCP.

## Acceptance Criteria

Deleted elements are absent from Scene; original_order and render_order are stable; id_map, bound_texts, bound_arrows, and frame_children are populated; linear abs_points match x/y plus relative points; normalize returns warnings without panics; unit tests cover every normalization step.
acceptance_criteria: Deleted elements are absent from Scene; original_order and render_order are stable; id_map, bound_texts, bound_arrows, and frame_children are populated; linear abs_points match x/y plus relative points; normalize returns warnings without panics; unit tests cover every normalization step.
close_reason: Implemented Scene normalization with deleted filtering, stable original/render order, id/frame/bound indexes, absolute linear points, basic bounds, background parsing, export padding, and normalization warnings with unit coverage.

title: Implement SVG node model, XML escaping, defs, and assembly
description:
Create the SVG foundation in excalidraw-render: SvgNode/SvgDef or equivalent typed builder, paint/style serialization, XML escaping, sanitized IDs, unique clipPath/def IDs, document viewBox/width/height calculation, background handling, and usvg-valid output. This foundation must support paths, text, rects, images, groups, clip paths, and embedded font/image defs.

## Acceptance Criteria

Rendered SVG parses through usvg; text and IDs are escaped; def references never point to missing IDs; background modes from-file/transparent/override work; smoke tests detect invalid XML, invalid path data, and missing defs.
acceptance_criteria: Rendered SVG parses through usvg; text and IDs are escaped; def references never point to missing IDs; background modes from-file/transparent/override work; smoke tests detect invalid XML, invalid path data, and missing defs.
close_reason: Implemented SVG document/node foundation with XML escaping, sanitized unique def IDs, def reference validation, background modes, viewBox assembly, render_svg smoke path, and usvg-backed tests.

title: Implement style resolution for stroke, fill, dash, opacity, roundness, and transforms
description:
Implement shared render style resolution for stroke color, background fill, opacity, stroke width, stroke style/dash arrays, roundness types, and rotation transforms around the element center. This prevents each element renderer from inventing its own interpretation of Excalidraw visual properties.

## Acceptance Criteria

All element renderers use shared style helpers; dashed/dotted styles are serialized correctly; opacity applies consistently to grouped child nodes; roundness types are handled for rectangles; rotation tests verify center-based transforms.
acceptance_criteria: All element renderers use shared style helpers; dashed/dotted styles are serialized correctly; opacity applies consistently to grouped child nodes; roundness types are handled for rectangles; rotation tests verify center-based transforms.

title: Expose parse, validate, and warning APIs in excalidraw-core
description:
Provide public core APIs such as parse_str, parse_reader or file helpers, validation report types, validation limits, scene warnings, and structured errors. Validation must be independently usable without rendering and should distinguish fatal errors from warnings: malformed JSON and hard limit violations are fatal; unknown or unsupported content and malformed optional fields become warnings when recoverable.

## Acceptance Criteria

parse_str returns typed files and structured errors; validate returns valid flag, element count, errors, and warnings; validation limits cover element count, payload size, text length, points, files, and image data URL bytes; tests cover corrupt JSON, hard limits, unknown elements, missing images, invalid colors, and unsupported elements.
acceptance_criteria: parse_str returns typed files and structured errors; validate returns valid flag, element count, errors, and warnings; validation limits cover element count, payload size, text length, points, files, and image data URL bytes; tests cover corrupt JSON, hard limits, unknown elements, missing images, invalid colors, and unsupported elements.
close_reason: Exposed parse_str/parse_slice/parse_reader, structured ParseError, ValidationLimits/Report/Error/Warning, validate_str/validate_file, and tests for corrupt JSON, hard limits, unknown/unsupported elements, missing images, invalid colors, and invalid indexes.

title: Implement rmcp stdio server and rendering tools
description:
Implement excalidraw-mcp using rmcp stdio transport with tools render_file, to_svg, to_png, parse_elements, describe_scene, and validate. Tools must call core/render APIs, include warnings and structured errors in every response, avoid panics on malformed inputs, and provide stable JSON fields useful to AI agents.

## Acceptance Criteria

excd serve starts an MCP stdio server; all planned tools are registered; render_file returns png_base64, dimensions, and warnings; parse/describe/validate are parse-only where appropriate; malformed input returns structured errors; integration test exercises the server with an MCP client or protocol harness.
acceptance_criteria: excd serve starts an MCP stdio server; all planned tools are registered; render_file returns png_base64, dimensions, and warnings; parse/describe/validate are parse-only where appropriate; malformed input returns structured errors; integration test exercises the server with an MCP client or protocol harness.

title: Implement excd CLI commands and warning output
description:
Implement the excalidraw-cli binary excd with commands view, convert, to-svg, to-png, info, describe, validate, and serve. CLI commands should call excalidraw-core/excalidraw-render APIs only, support scale/padding/background/quality/unsupported/warnings options, default to stdout for to-svg when output is omitted, default to input.png for to-png, and infer output format for convert.

## Acceptance Criteria

excd --help documents all commands; to-svg, to-png, convert, info, describe, validate, and serve route through shared APIs; warning output supports json/text/silent; invalid files return structured nonzero errors; integration tests exercise CLI behavior and output defaults.
acceptance_criteria: excd --help documents all commands; to-svg, to-png, convert, info, describe, validate, and serve route through shared APIs; warning output supports json/text/silent; invalid files return structured nonzero errors; integration tests exercise CLI behavior and output defaults.

title: Build SVG validity, visual regression, and accepted-deviation harness
description:
Create the renderer fidelity harness. Every rendered SVG should parse via usvg and valid XML checks. Visual regression should rasterize fixture SVG to PNG and compare against golden PNGs with tolerances for antialiasing. Selected official Excalidraw web exports should be used as oracle fixtures, and accepted deviations must be documented rather than hidden.

## Acceptance Criteria

SVG validity tests run for all render fixtures; visual regression supports per-quality baselines; comparison tolerates small antialiasing differences; accepted deviations are recorded in docs; each renderer bug fixed adds or updates a fixture; CI can run visual tests as a main/PR gate or documented soft gate initially.
acceptance_criteria: SVG validity tests run for all render fixtures; visual regression supports per-quality baselines; comparison tolerates small antialiasing differences; accepted deviations are recorded in docs; each renderer bug fixed adds or updates a fixture; CI can run visual tests as a main/PR gate or documented soft gate initially.

title: Add CI, formatting, lint, and test gates
description:
Add GitHub Actions and local commands that enforce Rust formatting, clippy, tests, and basic workspace health from the start. The project is correctness-first, so the pipeline should fail on compiler warnings and should make room for future SVG validity, visual regression, and benchmark jobs without requiring browser or Node dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria

CI runs cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test; workflow uses stable Rust; no browser/Node setup is required; failure logs are actionable; local README documents equivalent commands.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test; workflow uses stable Rust; no browser/Node setup is required; failure logs are actionable; local README documents equivalent commands.

title: Scaffold Rust workspace and crate boundaries for excd
description:
Create the foundational workspace for excalidraw-native: a root Cargo workspace with no root src directory, crates for excalidraw-core, excalidraw-render, excalidraw-cli, excalidraw-tui, and excalidraw-mcp, plus fixture/golden/integration-test directories. The scaffold must encode the architectural boundary from the plan: core owns parse/validate/normalize; render owns SVG/PNG and visual semantics; CLI/TUI/MCP are consumers and must not duplicate renderer logic. The CLI binary name is excd. Use a local path dependency on ../rough-rs during co-development and leave the release switch to crates.io explicit.

## Success Criteria

Workspace builds with cargo metadata; each crate has a minimal compiling lib/bin target; root has no src/; fixture, golden, and integration directories exist; README references the actual crate layout; cargo fmt --check and cargo check --all-targets pass.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace builds with cargo metadata; each crate has a minimal compiling lib/bin target; root has no src/; fixture, golden, and integration directories exist; README references the actual crate layout; cargo fmt --check and cargo check --all-targets pass.
close_reason: Implemented compiling Rust workspace scaffold with five crate boundaries, excd binary skeleton, fixture/golden/integration directories, README workspace notes, and clean cargo/UBS gates.

title: Run fidelity pass against Excalidraw web exports and patch renderer/rough-rs gaps
description:
After v0.1 renderer paths exist, compare output against official Excalidraw exports for normal diagrams. Patch excalidraw-render for Excalidraw-specific glue and patch rough-rs when a generally useful rough rendering primitive or behavior is missing. Preserve correctness over shortcuts, document known deviations, and add fixtures for each bug found.

## Success Criteria

Representative diagrams are compared against official exports; documented deviations exist for accepted differences; renderer patches include regression fixtures; rough-rs gaps are tracked and fixed upstream when general; no hidden browser dependency is introduced.
acceptance_criteria: Representative diagrams are compared against official exports; documented deviations exist for accepted differences; renderer patches include regression fixtures; rough-rs gaps are tracked and fixed upstream when general; no hidden browser dependency is introduced.

title: Create fixture and oracle corpus skeleton
description:
Create the fixture layout described in the plan: simple shapes, standalone text, bound text, basic and bound arrows, elbow arrows, freedraw, embedded images, frame clipping, complex diagrams, and a large 200-element file. The initial corpus may include synthetic minimal files, but the structure must support real Excalidraw exports and selected official web-export oracle images later.

## Acceptance Criteria

tests/fixtures includes representative .excalidraw files; tests/golden or tests/oracle has SVG/PNG subfolders; fixtures cover all v0.1 element categories including unsupported placeholders; fixture README documents source, intent, and whether each file is synthetic or real.
acceptance_criteria: tests/fixtures includes representative .excalidraw files; tests/golden or tests/oracle has SVG/PNG subfolders; fixtures cover all v0.1 element categories including unsupported placeholders; fixture README documents source, intent, and whether each file is synthetic or real.

title: Integrate rough-rs for hand-drawn rectangle, ellipse, and diamond rendering
description:
Wire rough-rs as the source of truth for hand-drawn geometry in Full and FastSvg quality modes. Implement rectangle, ellipse, and diamond rendering with Excalidraw-compatible roughness, bowing, stroke, fill style, stroke width, opacity, and seed. Clean mode should emit clean geometric primitives without rough path generation. Missing generally useful rough behavior should be fixed in rough-rs rather than duplicated here.

## Acceptance Criteria

Rectangle/ellipse/diamond render through rough-rs in Full/FastSvg; Clean mode uses clean SVG nodes; fill styles include hachure, cross-hatch, dots, dashed, zigzag-line, solid, and none where supported; same seed produces deterministic output; tests document any rough-rs gaps.
acceptance_criteria: Rectangle/ellipse/diamond render through rough-rs in Full/FastSvg; Clean mode uses clean SVG nodes; fill styles include hachure, cross-hatch, dots, dashed, zigzag-line, solid, and none where supported; same seed produces deterministic output; tests document any rough-rs gaps.

title: Map v0.2 Mermaid diagram types to Excalidraw elements
description:
Implement conversion for Tier 1 Mermaid diagrams: flowchart, sequence, class, state, and ER. Map shapes to Excalidraw rectangles/ellipses/diamonds/frames, routed edges to arrow points and labels, classes to text blocks, sequence actors/lifelines/messages/activations/notes, state starts/ends/composites/choices/forks, and ER cardinalities to crowfoot arrowheads. Tier 2 diagrams should use placeholder behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

Fixtures cover flowchart, sequence, class, state, and ER mappings; edge labels become bound text; subgraphs/namespaces/composites become frames; unsupported Tier 2 diagrams produce documented fallback; generated Excalidraw files validate and render.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover flowchart, sequence, class, state, and ER mappings; edge labels become bound text; subgraphs/namespaces/composites become frames; unsupported Tier 2 diagrams produce documented fallback; generated Excalidraw files validate and render.

title: Define renderer public API, options, warnings, and error model
description:
Expose RenderOptions, RenderQuality, BackgroundMode, UnsupportedElementMode, ImagePolicy, TextPolicy, RenderOutput, RenderWarning, and RenderError. Keep dark mode out of core render options. All render functions must return warnings alongside output where possible and must make unsupported/missing content behavior explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria

render_svg and render_png signatures return RenderOutput<T>; options include scale, padding, background, quality, unsupported, image_policy, and text_policy; unsupported placeholder/skip/error modes are represented; render errors and warnings are structured and tested.
acceptance_criteria: render_svg and render_png signatures return RenderOutput<T>; options include scale, padding, background, quality, unsupported, image_policy, and text_policy; unsupported placeholder/skip/error modes are represented; render errors and warnings are structured and tested.

title: Add correctness-aware benchmarks for library, CLI, and MCP paths
description:
Add benchmark groups from the plan without letting performance override correctness: parse_lib, normalize_lib, render_svg_lib, render_png_lib, cli_e2e, and mcp_e2e. Track targets and fail thresholds separately, starting with soft warnings if needed. Library benchmarks and process-spawn benchmarks must remain separate.

## Acceptance Criteria

Benchmarks cover simple/complex/large fixtures; CLI e2e includes process startup and file IO; MCP e2e measures stdio request/response; reports compare against target and fail-threshold values; benchmarks do not require browser or Node.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmarks cover simple/complex/large fixtures; CLI e2e includes process startup and file IO; MCP e2e measures stdio request/response; reports compare against target and fail-threshold values; benchmarks do not require browser or Node.
